taskSummary: Convert the study session route to Nunjucks/GOV.UK/global Sass, use current id/session route parameters with legacy fallback only on the session route, and make participant consent plus session notes D1-backed with Airtable fallback only.
implementationHighlights: Added a generated Nunjucks/GOV.UK study-session page and removed the page-specific session stylesheet.
Kept study-session styles in global study-page Sass.
Loaded participants through the D1-canonical participants API instead of static mocks.
Made participant consent and session notes D1-first with Airtable fallback only.
Used GOV.UK summary lists and error summary patterns for consent and participant-load states.
Kept saved notes hidden until a note exists.
Attached the full seven-button rich-text toolbar to the note editor as a compact row on small screens, using a larger optically centred typographic double quote for blockquote.
Adjusted study session GOV.UK summary-list rows so labels and values use the full datalist width.

Follow-up review fixes added authenticated API fetches, routed session note saves through the configured API origin, removed legacy sid as a session-note id fallback, and fell back to Airtable when the D1 participant consent cache is empty.
Updated the D1 migration ordering policy to identify 0024 as the next main migration prefix after 0023_session_consent_and_notes.sql.
Updated the participant consent page contract so it excludes the Back to Study action and does not load the extra GOV.UK table stylesheet.

validation.result: passed with existing warnings and no errors
passed with 272 tests passing and 0 failing

passed: seven toolbar controls remained visible in one compact row, toolbar was attached to the editor, blockquote used a larger centred typographic double quote, no horizontal overflow, consent summary used full-width summary-list rows, saved notes appeared only after saving, and participant-load failure rendered in the GOV.UK error summary

residualRisks: The populated local preview uses fixture/static responses; deployed behavior depends on applying the D1 migration and having the RESEARCHOPS_D1 binding available.
Airtable is fallback-only by design; if D1 and Airtable are both unavailable, service endpoints return explicit unavailable responses.
